title: Hardcoded timestamp=0.0 in IoT sensor readings
description: DualSensorPair.sample() sets timestamp=0.0 on every sensor reading. No real wall-clock time is ever recorded, making all temporal analysis (trends, correlations, time-of-day patterns) impossible. This defeats the purpose of time-series sensor data entirely.
impact: All temporal analysis, correlations, and time-of-day pattern detection are impossible. The entire IoT data pipeline produces untimestamped noise.

title: Timestamp=0.0 in actuator commands
description: ActuatorCommand carries no real timestamp. All actuator events appear to happen at epoch zero, making audit trails, latency measurement, and time-based debugging impossible.
impact: No audit trail for barrier actions, no way to measure sensor-to-actuator latency, and no way to correlate actuator events with external events.

title: FIFO eviction in IPFS cache, not LRU
description: IPFSOffChainStore uses an OrderedDict for caching but never calls move_to_end() on get(). Eviction removes the oldest inserted item regardless of how frequently it is accessed. Despite being described as an LRU cache, it behaves as pure FIFO.
impact: Frequently accessed items are evicted sooner than expected, degrading cache hit rate and defeating the purpose of an LRU caching layer.

title: IPFS store entirely in-memory with no persistence
description: IPFSOffChainStore keeps all data in a plain Python dict with no serialization to disk or database. Every pinned CID and stored blob is lost on process restart.
impact: All off-chain data is ephemeral. Restarting the server loses every stored parking session, blocking proof, and pinned document.

title: IPFS pin set never shrinks (memory leak)
description: The pin() method unconditionally adds to _pinned but there is no unpin() or eviction mechanism. Pinned CIDs accumulate indefinitely, causing unbounded memory growth.
impact: Long-running processes will exhaust memory as pinned CIDs accumulate without bound.

title: 64-bit CID collision risk via birthday paradox
description: CIDs are truncated to the first 16 hex characters (64 bits). The birthday paradox gives a 50% collision probability after approximately 2^32 inserts, risking silent data corruption.
impact: Different parking sessions can silently map to the same truncated CID, causing data lookup to return the wrong blob.

title: PoW mining has no nonce upper bound (infinite loop risk)
description: Block.mine() increments nonce without any upper bound. If the configured difficulty is unreachable (e.g., all-zero target after hash), the loop spins forever with no timeout or progress check.
impact: A single mine() call can hang the process indefinitely, blocking all ledger operations and effectively causing a denial of service.

title: MAX_CHAIN_LENGTH defined but never enforced
description: The constant MAX_CHAIN_LENGTH exists but mine_pending() only logs a warning when the chain exceeds it. The chain continues to grow unbounded regardless of the limit.
impact: Chain length grows without bound over time, degrading get_balance and chain-validation performance and eventually exhausting memory.

title: get_balance performs O(n*m) full chain scan
description: get_balance scans every block in the entire chain, then every transaction in each block. There is no index on driver_id, no ledger checkpoint, and no balance cache. Each call is O(num_blocks * avg_tx_per_block).
impact: As the chain grows, balance lookups become progressively slower. At 10K+ blocks this will be a performance bottleneck for every API call that checks balances.

title: PoolManager create() persists but allocate() and release() do not
description: PoolManager.create() writes to persistence, but allocate() and release() only modify in-memory state. After a restart, allocations are lost while lots exist, creating phantom available spots.
impact: Server restart causes all active allocations to vanish. Drivers who had reserved spots find them double-booked, and over-allocation becomes possible.

title: ParkingTransaction dataclass defined but unused
description: The ParkingTransaction dataclass is defined in transaction.py but BlockchainLedger stores transactions as raw dicts. The dataclass is never instantiated anywhere in the codebase.
impact: Dead code that adds maintenance burden and confuses readers about the actual transaction representation.

title: No time-based spot expiry in ParkingPool
description: ParkingPool stores end_time for every allocation but never checks it. There is no background sweep, no expiry check on lookup, and no timeout. Allocations remain active forever unless explicitly released.
impact: Drivers who leave without explicitly releasing their spot cause permanent capacity loss. Over time every spot becomes exhausted.

title: Contract ignores floating-point rounding in distributions
description: RevenueShareContract hardcodes remaining=0.0 but floating-point multiplication means the sum of distributed shares may not equal the original price. Pennies are silently lost or gained.
impact: Accumulated rounding errors across thousands of transactions cause financial drift in settlement totals.

title: Smart contracts have no caller authorization
description: SmartContract.execute() never verifies the caller's identity or permissions. Any component or user can execute any contract function without restriction.
impact: Unauthorized actors can execute arbitrary contract logic, including revenue distribution and rate changes, without authentication.

title: Feature column name mismatch between engine and builder
description: engine.py produces a column named pe_net_flux but builder.py X_COLS and constants.py EXPECTED_FEATURE_COLS both reference net_flux. Offline training data will have the wrong column name, causing inference to fail at runtime with KeyError or silently use wrong features.
impact: The ML pipeline silently uses mismatched feature columns. Inference crashes or produces incorrect predictions because the trained model expects different column names.

title: Duplicate feature column definitions across modules
description: X_COLS in builder.py and EXPECTED_FEATURE_COLS in constants.py are identical lists defining the same feature schema. Any change to one requires manually updating the other, creating a drift risk.
impact: Future edits may update only one copy, leading to silent train/serve skew that is difficult to debug.

title: Three different price-elasticity constants across subsystems
description: The digital twin simulator uses 0.8, the RL environment uses 0.8, but the MARL ZoneEnvironment uses 0.15 (clipped to 0.1-0.3). These subsystems model demand response to price differently, giving inconsistent simulation results.
impact: Simulation and RL training operate on different demand models. A policy that works in the RL environment may fail in the digital twin or vice versa.

title: RL state dimension always returns 0.5 (never updates)
description: state[2] is hardcoded to 0.5 and never written to by any component (ParkingControlEnv, NeuralAgent, or QMIXMARL). This state dimension carries no information and wastes neural-network capacity.
impact: The RL agent uses a meaningless constant feature dimension, wasting model capacity and potentially confusing the policy if it tries to learn from it.

title: Heuristic fallback in NeuralAgent when model not fitted
description: NeuralAgent.act() with train=True falls through to a heuristic baseline if epsilon does not trigger and the model has not been fitted yet. This is a rare code path but represents a silent mode switch.
impact: During early training steps the agent silently uses heuristics instead of neural-network exploration, potentially biasing early experience replay.

title: Generator._mse_grad returns zero gradients (dead/broken code)
description: _mse_grad always returns zero-valued gradients. It is never called because train_step inlines its own gradient computation. The method exists only as dead code that would produce bogus gradients if invoked.
impact: If anyone calls _mse_grad directly, they will get zero gradients and training will not converge.

title: is_false_positive flag is semantically misnamed
description: The is_false_positive flag is set when ultrasonic and vision sensors disagree, not through comparison against ground truth. True false-positive rate is unknown. The flag conflates sensor disagreement with the statistical concept of a false positive.
impact: Operators misled into thinking the system measures false-positive rate, while it actually only measures inter-sensor disagreement.

title: Double toggle in ActuatorBridge can leave barrier open when restricted
description: High occupancy sets restricted=True (is_open=False), then rl_action<-0.05 sets reservation_only=False (is_open=True). These two code paths can undo each other, potentially leaving a restricted barrier open.
impact: A restricted barrier may be left open, allowing unauthorized entry during high-occupancy periods.

title: Two database sessions opened per authenticated request
description: get_current_user opens its own database session, then the route handler calls get_db to open a second. Two separate sessions are created for every single authenticated request.
impact: Database connection pool is exhausted twice as fast as necessary, limiting concurrent users unnecessarily.

title: LotCreate schema allows price_cap < base_price
description: The LotCreate Pydantic schema does not validate that price_cap >= base_price. A misconfigured lot can have a cap below its base rate, which breaks pricing logic downstream.
impact: API clients can create parking lots with nonsensical pricing (cap below base), causing undefined behavior in pricing and RL components.

title: Flat-rate sessions discard pipeline-computed dynamic price
description: session_service.py overwrites entry_price with lot.base_price after the pipeline has already computed a dynamic price via ML, RL, and blockchain components. All that computation is wasted for flat-rate sessions.
impact: Dynamic pricing CPU cycles are wasted on flat-rate lots, and flat-rate sessions ignore the price that demand prediction computed.

title: Sensor role is unreachable through registration
description: Ingestion routes check for a sensor role, but auth.py register() always sets role='driver'. Sensor credentials can only be created by direct database injection.
impact: Sensor role is effectively dead code. The sensor ingestion endpoints are unreachable through normal API usage.

title: Model version hardcoded in database.py while sessions.py uses env var
description: PredictionMetric.model_version defaults to 'rf+xgb_ensemble_v2' as a string literal in database.py, but sessions.py reads MODEL_VERSION from environment variables. These two sources of truth will inevitably diverge.
impact: Logged prediction metrics can have a different version string than the actual deployed model, making metric analysis unreliable.

title: threading.Lock() used instead of RLock risks deadlock
description: SlotStateEngine uses threading.Lock() (non-reentrant). The _cleanup_batch method acquires the lock, and methods that call _cleanup_batch then try to acquire the lock again. Currently safe due to call ordering but fragile under future refactoring.
impact: A simple reordering of internal calls or addition of a helper that acquires the lock will cause a hard deadlock.

title: SlotStateLog.lot_id (String(20)) truncates ParkingLot.lot_id (String(50))
description: SlotStateLog defines lot_id as String(20) while ParkingLot.lot_id is String(50). Lot IDs longer than 20 characters are silently truncated on insert, causing log entries to point to the wrong lot.
impact: Long lot IDs are silently truncated, corrupting the audit trail and making slot state logs point to incorrect lots.

title: Expected edge module (AnomalyDetector, EdgeSimulator, UploadBuffer) does not exist
description: The codebase references or expects an src/edge/ directory containing AnomalyDetector, EdgeSimulator, and UploadBuffer components. This directory and all its expected files are entirely absent.
impact: Any code path that imports from or references the edge module will fail with ImportError. The edge-computing layer of the architecture is completely missing.
